front: Opera: Manon sings 'Adieu, notre petite table' when she decides to leave Des Grieux for Monsieur de Bretigny
Opera: Manon also sings the seduction aria 'N'est-ce plus ma main' before being arrested for cheating at gambling
Opera: title character dies in Des Grieux's arms while being marched to Le Havre for deportation
Ballet: 1974 Kenneth MacMillan work based on this character uses Massenet's music but not his opera; the 'Elegie' underscores the pas de deux with Des Grieux
Opera: Act 1 tenor aria 'Donna non vidi mai' sung by Des Grieux upon meeting Manon, who is heading to a convent
Opera: Act 2 aria 'In quelle trine morbide' — Manon complains that her current lover (Geronte) is boring
Opera: closing aria 'Sola, perduta, abbandonata' ('lonely, lost, and abandoned') as the title character nears death in a Louisiana desert
Opera: intermezzo between Acts 2 and 3 represents the protagonist's journey to Le Havre; a character's thoughts are written into the score during it
Opera: the composer dismissed a similar work on the same subject as full of 'powder and minuets,' despite including his own minuet in Act 2
Opera: the finale draws on a string quartet the composer wrote for the funeral of the Duke of Aosta
